frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Sequential integration of decision-relevant evidence toward a final judgment

frameworks.name: confirmation bias
frameworks.one_line: Selective down-weighting of post-choice evidence inconsistent with initial choice

core_question: How do intermittent binary choices alter the accumulation and temporal weighting of subsequent decision evidence?
key_assumptions: Evidence is integrated over a protracted stream toward a final estimation
Intermittent choices can be dissociated from choice-independent motor responses
Perceptual and numerical evidence engage comparable accumulation mechanisms
manipulations: Intermittent response type: binary choice vs choice-independent motor control
Timing of intermittent response within evidence stream
key_findings: Intermittent choice reduced sensitivity to subsequent evidence
Choice flipped the relative temporal weighting of early vs late evidence in final estimation
Choice-induced non-selective sensitivity reduction predicted selective down-weighting of choice-inconsistent evidence
Effects held across both perceptual and numerical tasks
interpretations: Active decisions modulate ongoing evidence accumulation dynamics
A choice-triggered active mechanism produces confirmation-bias-like selective re-weighting
